title: Define Zephyr Kconfig and devicetree model for HAL capabilities
description: Why this exists: partner board ports should be mostly overlays, Kconfig fragments, and driver bindings, not application rewrites. We need a stable way to express board capabilities and feature gates. What needs to be done: define Kconfig symbols and devicetree conventions for LICHEN board capabilities: CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_DISPLAY, HAS_BUTTONS, HAS_LEDS, HAS_GNSS, HAS_BATTERY, HAS_PMIC, HAS_BLE_LOCAL, HAS_SERIAL_LOCAL, HAS_EXTERNAL_FLASH, radio model selection, UI profile, and source-provider support for location/time. Ensure runtime capability discovery can be derived from build-time config where appropriate. Acceptance criteria: Kconfig options and devicetree aliases/chosen nodes are documented; sample overlays exist for reference boards or fake/native test targets; invalid combinations fail at build time where possible; headless builds do not pull display/UI code; two code-review passes are completed before closure.
notes: Progress from project-LICHEN-ev5b.3: added CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_* capability symbols, radio model choices with compatible validation, UI/location/time provider choices, serial chosen conventions, GPIO LED/button alias validation, and native_sim default/loopback HAL tests. Follow-up project-LICHEN-6mco tracks missing positive serial-local precedence test. Keeping h7t5.3 open because broader reference board overlays/docs and additional invalid-combination test fixtures remain.
